def: vocative plural of بولی (bolī)

ety: bol˚ = Prk. वोल्ल(इ), or लोल(इ); S. वद(ति), rt. वद्; or, prob. S. ब्रूय(ते), pass. of rt. ब्रू, corrupted to बूर्य(ते)
def: v.n. To speak, talk, tell, say, utter, pronounce, declare, give utterance to; to bid (at an auction); to cry (one's wares); to give forth sound, to sound, to bark (as a dog, &c.), chirp (as a bird, and the like, according to the animal); to strike (as a clock, &c.); to crack (with a sound), split, break; to be brisk or active (as a market)
more: v. n. 1. To sound; give or emit sound. 2. E. To strike (the hour). Chār bole ānā . 3. To crack; break; smash. 4. To chirp; chirrup; twitter. Chiṛīmār ṭolā, bhāṅt bhāṅt kā jānvar bolā . Prov. It is a bird-mart where voices diverse you may hear. (A concourse various, opinions various). 5. To speak; talk; open one's lips; give tongue or utterance to; break silence. Phr. To have a tongue in one's head; to fall from, or escape one's lips; to speak one's mind. Bolī bolīṅ, to ye bolīṅ, "merī jūtī bole." Prov. If she e'er spoke at all, it was, 'my shoes may speak.' 6. To tell; say; declare; assert. 7. To cry one's wares ( āwāz lagānā ). 8. To bid; make a bid (at an auction). 9. ( Rus .) To call; hail; summon; invite. Wā he bol le nā? Rus. Why dont you call out to him? 10. To prescribe; lay down; direct. Pahre pe kiskī naukrī bolī gaī? 11. To answer; reply; respond. 1. Wāṅ jā-ke kyā tum bologe? S…

(perhaps, from s. r. वद Speak) v. n. 1. To speak, talk. 2. To sound, emit sound, to articulate. 3. To tell, to say. بول اٿهنا bol uṭhnā, To speak out, to exclaim. بولناچالنا bolnā-chālnā, To converse, speak together. بولي سوبولنا bole so bolnā, To speak repeatedly.
subs: v.n. To leave word; to order (as goods, &c.)
v.n. To speak out, exclaim, cry out
v.n. To leave word (with, or at); to be proclaimed (on change), to be bankrupt; to be over, come to an end; to become; old or worn out, give signs of decay, &c.; to crack, split, break (as a vessel)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀩𑁄𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀇 (bŏllaï). Also compare بُلَانَا (bulānā, “to call”).

def: oblique infinitive of بولنا
adj. (f. -ī), Saying; speaking, talking (e.g. boltī mainā, 'a talking mainā); — s.m. Faculty of speech; life, soul; a thing which sounds; pipe or ḥuqqa (in the language of faqīrs).
more: n. m. 1. The organ or faculty of speech. 2. Life; soul; the inner man ( boltā pursh ). 1. Ye jab boltā apnā so jāëgā, Yahīṅ apnā pyālah bhī ho-jāëgā . Inshā. When this my life shall rest in sleep, My funeral feast here will they keep. 2. Boltā hai jab talak hai boltā . Prov. While there is life there is speech. 3. Bolte kī āshnāī hai . Prov. Attachment ends with life. 4. Bolte ko jān le, pahchān le, Boltā jo kuchh kahe so mān le . Mendicant's cry. Your soul right know, discern it, pray, Whate'er the soul doth say, its voice obey. 3. A huqqā or pipe. (Faqīrs' idiom.) Safar meṅ boltā sāth ho, to jahāṅ chāhā chilam bhar lī . Your huqqā in your journeys take, at will, your chilam filled, your smoke you take.
adj. Speaking; talking. Boltā chākar munīb ke āge gūṅgā . Prov. Your servant fluent though with all to speak. Before his master will strict silence keep.
part. act. (from بولنا used substantively) The faculty of speech; soul, life.

def: (dakh.) See واويلا wā wailā.

n. m. S. वृहस्पति lord of increase, Pr. bhaapphaī . 1. A name of the spiritual preceptor of the Devatȧs , corresponding to the Thor of the Northmen, and the Jupiter of the ancient Greeks and Romans. 2. (Ast.) The planet Jupiter (A. mushtarī ). brahaspat , adj. Learned ( ālim ); deep-read; erudite. Āve na jāve, brahaspat kahlāve . Prov. Nought comes to him nor from him, yet Brahaspat (the bright planet) he calls himself. brahaspat-vār,—bār, birhaspat, biraspat, bispat , W. Hin. bīphe, bīphai, bephaë , E. Hin. and Gaṛh.; biphaīyā , Bhoj. n. m. Thursday ( jumerāt ).

n. m. varan a syllable. A term in prosody. It consists of four metrical lines, or charans counted by syllables. It is of three kinds— 1. Sam even, having an equal number of syllables in each line. 2. ardh-sam half even, having an equal number of syllables in the first and third, and second and fourth lines. 3. bisham odd, having a different number of syllables in each line.

def: v.n. more: v. n. S. अभ्युत्तेजयति (?). 1. To be extinguished, or put out (fire, a light, etc.). 2. To be quenched (thirst); be satisfied (hunger, passion). 3. To be dispirited, disheartened, discouraged. Lāt lāt meṅ murg bujh jātā hai . 4. To droop; hang down (the head); to be downcast. Shām se kuchh bujhā sā rahtā hai, Dil huā hai chirāg muflis kā . Mīr. At eventide my heart begins to droop and fade, As gives the poor man's lamp a dim half light, half shade. 5. To be tired, weary or sick of. Pānī tabīb de hai hameṅ, kyā bujhā huā, Hai dil hī zindagī se hamārā bujhā huā . Zauq. 6. To be well fried or browned. 1. Kachauriyoṅ kā ghān bujh gayā . 2. Kitnā sāg chaṛhāyā thā, bujh-kar itnā sā rah gayā . Wom. 7. To have a heated piece of iron, etc. thrust into water. Bujhā-huā pānī . 8. To be tempered (steel). Zahr kī bujhī-hui talwār . 9. To be shuffled (the cards).

def: v.t. To understand, perceive, make out, comprehend; to conceive, think, consider, regard, account; to ascertain, inquire (of, se)
more: v. a. 1. To understand; comprehend ( samajhnā ). Yeh to būjhe Lāl Bujhakkaṛ, aur na būjhe koë, "Pairoṅ chākī bāṅdh-ke kahīṅ hirnā kūdā hoë ." This Lāl Bujhakkaṛ only can make out, no other can. "A mill-stone tied to his feet a bounding deer hath ran." ☞ Lāl Bujhakkaṛ was a celebrated fool among fools whose explanations of strange phenomena never failed to find acceptance. The above couplet refers to the footmarks of an elephant who had passed through during the night. 2. To ask; enquire ( pūchhnā ). Rus. Bāppū se būjh āūn̄ . W. Rus. 3. To account; rate; consider; regard; esteem.

def: adj. See s.v. A. bū. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

Bulāë na chalāë, maiṅ to dulhan kī chachī . Prov. Nor called nor wanted, 'I am the bride's aunt.' 2. To summon; subpœnā ; require one's presence. 3. To sound; produce a sound. Huqqā bulānā .

subs: v.t. To call, &c. = bulānā; (and intens) to call off or away.

def: A historic monument in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India to commemorate the Mughal conquest of Gujarat.
Bulandshahr (a city in Uttar Pradesh, India); Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see بُلَنْد (buland), شَہَر (śahar).
wety: Renaming of its original name, بَرَن (baran), to literally, “high city” due to its elevation of 195 metres on the Indo-Gangetic Plain.

def: adj. High, lofty, tall; elevated, exalted, sublime; loud (syn. ūṅćā)
more: adj. High. See. اونچا (1—5). buland-hausalā , adj. Aspiring; ambitious; enterprising; large-minded. buland kar'nā , v. a. See اونچانا buland honā , v. n. To mount; rise up; ascend; to be elevated. buland jagah , n. f. ( Ast .) Parallax.

adj. High, elevated, lofty, exalted, sublime, tall, loud. بلندپايه buland-pāya, adj. Of high rank. بلندحوصله buland-ḥauṣila, adj. Ambitious, aspiring. بلندرتبه يامرتبه buland-rutba or -martaba, High in rank or dignity. بلندکرنا buland karnā, To lift, elevate, raise up. بلندهونا buland honā, To mount, rise up, become high, ascend. بلندنظر buland-naz̤ar, adj. Aspiring. بلندهمت buland-himmat, adj. Ambitious, high-minded. بلندهمتي buland-himmat-ī, s. f. High-mindedness, ambition.
subs: adj. Ambitious, aspiring, enterprising; large-minded, magnanimous
buland martaba, adj. Of high rank or dignity; high in rank, &c.
v.t. To lift, raise, raise up, elevate (syn. ūṅćā karnā, uṅćānā)
adj. Of lofty intention, high-minded, ambitious; — buland-himmatī, s.f. High-mindedness; magnanimity; ambition
v.n. To rise up, become high, ascend, mount, soar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُلند (buland). Compare Punjabi ਬੁਲੰਦ (buland) / بُلَند (buland), Gujarati બુલંદ (buland).

def: s.f. Height, elevation, loftiness, eminence, exaltation; pride; loudness; high land.
more: n. f. 1. Height. See اُنچائی 2. Pride. Bakht uṛ gayā, bulandī rah gaī . Prov. His grandness gone, his loftiness remains, 3. ( Ast .) Prominence ( uṅchāī ).
s. f. Height, elevation, eminence, loftiness, exaltation, loudness.
